insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 191, 'Tips for the fastest way to get from Hai Phong to trekking spots in Cat Ba.', '

The fastest way to get from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots is to use the Cat Hai – Phu Long Cable Car (a 10-minute aerial crossing) or the high-speed boat at Binh Wharf/Got Wharf. Once on the island, you should rent a motorbike or grab a taxi straight to the gate of Cat Ba National Park to save as much time and energy as possible for the climb.

Why you need to know the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots

Knowing the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots gives you major advantages:

  • Save energy: Long journeys and tiring waits will drain your stamina before you even start the challenging trekking route.
  • Avoid traffic jams: In peak season, the Got ferry terminal can be backed up for hours. Knowing shortcuts or alternative transport is the key to escaping the crowds.
  • More time to experience: Arriving earlier means more time to explore the Kim Giao forest, Lien Minh Valley, or Viet Hai village.

 

Which means of transport get you from Hai Phong to Cat Ba at high speed?

To apply the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots, you need to drop the traditional ferry mindset during rush hours. Here are the optimal options:

1. Cat Hai – Phu Long Cable Car: The No. 1 choice for speed

This is the most modern and fastest option available today. Instead of spending 20-30 minutes on the ferry (not counting queue time), the cable car carries you across the sea in just about 8-10 minutes.

  • Advantages: No traffic worries, panoramic views of the bay from above, and super-fast transit.
  • How to go: From central Hai Phong, drive across Tan Vu Bridge to the Cat Hai cable car station. Buy a ticket and ride straight over to Phu Long station. At Phu Long, buses or taxis are waiting to take you into the center of the island or directly to the National Park.
  • Note: The cable car has a midday break and fixed operating hours, so check the schedule before you go.

2. High-speed boat at Binh Wharf/Got Wharf

If you don't have a personal vehicle and want to go straight from central Hai Phong, the high-speed boat is a fastest travel tip from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots well worth considering.

  • Hydrofoil (Mekong Hoang Yen, CatBa Island...): Departs from Binh Wharf or Got Wharf and runs straight to the central Cat Ba boat terminal. Travel time is about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
  • Advantage for trekkers: The boat docks right in the town center, where you can easily rent a motorbike or buy supplies before heading into the forest.

3. All-inclusive tourist bus combos

Many bus companies such as Daiichi and Good Morning Cat Ba offer through-tickets running directly from Hanoi or Hai Phong. They get priority crossing by ferry (or their own high-speed boat) without having to queue like private vehicles. This is the most "hassle-free" way to travel.

Detailed guide on connecting from the boat terminal/cable car to the trekking spots

After setting foot on Cat Ba Island, the next leg is what really matters. Your destination is the forest entrance (the National Park), not just the town. Below are the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots for this second leg.

From Phu Long Station (Cable car/Cai Vieng ferry) to the National Park

The distance from here to the National Park gate is about 15-20km.

  • Bus: There is a public bus route running along the cross-island road. It's cheap but you have to wait.
  • Taxi/Motorbike taxi: This is the fastest way. If traveling in a large group, call a taxi. If traveling alone, a motorbike taxi is the more flexible choice. It takes only about 20 minutes to reach the Park gate.

From Cat Ba town center to the trekking spots

If you take the high-speed boat into the center, you need to travel back about 13km to reach the National Park.

  • Rent a motorbike: Around 100k - 150k/day. This is the most popular option among trekkers because you can park at the forest gate and pick the bike up when you come back out.
  • Electric car: If traveling in a large group, you can charter an electric car to take you straight to the foot of the mountain.

In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

Specific trekking routes and the fastest way to reach them

To apply the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots effectively, you must know exactly which route you want to hike. Each route has a different starting point.

1. Kim Giao Forest – Ngu Lam Peak trekking route

This is the most popular route — easy to walk and with the best views.

  • Starting point: The main gate of Cat Ba National Park.
  • Fastest way to get there: From Hai Phong -> Cable car -> Taxi to the Park gate (headquarters). Buy an entrance ticket and start climbing right away. No need to stop in town unless necessary.

2. Ao Ech (Frog Pond) – Viet Hai village trekking route

This route is longer and more challenging (about 8-10km of forest trail).

  • Starting point: Also from the National Park headquarters, or via a side trail off the cross-island road.
  • Optimization tip: You should ride to the Park gate and trek through the forest to Viet Hai village. At Viet Hai, instead of walking back (which is very time-consuming), hire a boat or speedboat from Viet Hai pier back to Beo Wharf (Cat Ba town). This is a "hidden tip" that lets you complete a full loop without backtracking, saving time on your return to the mainland.

3. Lien Minh Valley – Butterfly season route

  • Starting point: Lien Minh hamlet, Tran Chau commune.
  • How to reach it: From the cross-island road, you need to turn onto the small road leading into Tran Chau commune. Renting a motorbike is the fastest travel tip from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots for this route, since the road into the hamlet is quite narrow and large buses struggle to go deep.

 

Common mistakes that slow you down

Even with the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots in hand, many people still make mistakes:

  • Riding a personal motorbike across the Got ferry on weekends: This is the classic mistake. You can get stuck for 2-3 hours under the hot sun. If you must ride a motorbike, set off very early (before 7am), or choose to park it and take the cable car/high-speed boat.
  • Not checking the tide schedule (for the Viet Hai route): If you plan to take a boat back from Viet Hai, remember that boats depend on the tide. If the water is too low, the boat can't dock and you'll be stranded.
  • Trusting Google Maps completely: In the forest or on some shortcuts in Cat Ba, Google Maps can give wrong directions. Ask locals or contact tour operators who know the terrain well.

 

Frequently asked questions about getting to Cat Ba trekking

To give you a fuller picture of the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots, I've compiled the most common questions below:

Can I bring a bicycle or motorbike on the cable car?

Currently, the Cat Hai – Phu Long cable car only carries passengers and does not transport motorbikes or bicycles. If you want to bring a mountain bike to combine trekking with cycling, you must take the Got ferry.

How long is the total travel time from Hai Phong to the forest entrance?

If you follow the right route: car to the cable car station (30 min) + cable car (10 min) + taxi to the Park gate (20 min), it takes only about 60 minutes to reach the trekking spot. That's much faster than the 2-3 hours of the traditional ferry.

Which season is best for Cat Ba trekking for the smoothest travel?

Autumn (September - November) and early summer (March - May) are ideal. During these times the weather is dry, the trails aren't slippery, and especially the number of domestic tourists isn't as overwhelming as in June and July, making travel by both ferry and cable car quick.

How much does the fastest route cost?

To optimize speed using the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots, the cost will be a bit higher than the ferry. A round-trip cable car ticket is around 300k (depending on the time), plus taxi/bus fare. However, this amount is well worth it to buy back time and a better experience.

A 5-step super-fast travel process for trekkers

Below is a summary of the standard process to help you picture the route more clearly:

  • Step 1: Set off from Hai Phong and head straight to the Cat Hai cable car station (don't turn into the Got ferry terminal).
  • Step 2: Buy a ticket and board the cable car, enjoying the 10-minute flight over the bay.
  • Step 3: At Phu Long station, immediately grab a waiting taxi or bus. Ask the driver to take you straight to the "Cat Ba National Park Gate."
  • Step 4: At the Park gate, buy an entrance ticket and drop off any heavy items (if needed).
  • Step 5: Start your trek up Ngu Lam Peak or into Ao Ech right away.

Mastering the fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots not only saves you time but also improves the quality of your trip. Instead of getting worn out waiting for transport, you'll have all your energy to conquer the majestic limestone peaks and breathe in the fresh air of this world biosphere reserve.

Remember, a successful trek starts with a smart travel plan. We hope these tips will help you have an exciting and fast journey to conquer Cat Ba.

If you're looking for a reputable provider to organize trekking tours combined with healing, or need more detailed itinerary advice, don't hesitate to contact us.

Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !

Phone: 0986662282

Email: support@virenaretreat.com

Website: www.virenaretreat.com

They get priority crossing by ferry (or their own high-speed boat) without having to queue like private vehicles. This is the most "hassle-free" way to travel.</span></p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-2.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="473" /></span></p>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Detailed guide on connecting from the boat terminal/cable car to the trekking spots</span></h2>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">After setting foot on Cat Ba Island, the next leg is what really matters. Your destination is the forest entrance (the National Park), not just the town. Below are the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> for this second leg.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">From Phu Long Station (Cable car/Cai Vieng ferry) to the National Park</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">The distance from here to the National Park gate is about 15-20km.</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Bus: There is a public bus route running along the cross-island road. It's cheap but you have to wait.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Taxi/Motorbike taxi: This is the fastest way. If traveling in a large group, call a taxi. If traveling alone, a motorbike taxi is the more flexible choice. It takes only about 20 minutes to reach the Park gate.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">From Cat Ba town center to the trekking spots</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">If you take the high-speed boat into the center, you need to travel back about 13km to reach the National Park.</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Rent a motorbike: Around 100k - 150k/day. This is the most popular option among trekkers because you can park at the forest gate and pick the bike up when you come back out.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Electric car: If traveling in a large group, you can charter an electric car to take you straight to the foot of the mountain.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <p><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; color: #e74c3c; background-color: #ffffff; vertical-align: baseline;">In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at</span><a href="http://vietnamtoure.com/"><span style="font-size: 13pt; color: #e74c3c; background-color: #ffffff; vertical-align: baseline;"> Vietnamtoure.com</span></a></span></p>\r\n
    <p><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-1.png" alt="" width="900" height="600" /></span></p>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Specific trekking routes and the fastest way to reach them</span></h2>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">To apply the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> effectively, you must know exactly which route you want to hike. Each route has a different starting point.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">1. Kim Giao Forest – Ngu Lam Peak trekking route</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">This is the most popular route — easy to walk and with the best views.</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Starting point: The main gate of Cat Ba National Park.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Fastest way to get there: From Hai Phong -> Cable car -> Taxi to the Park gate (headquarters). Buy an entrance ticket and start climbing right away. No need to stop in town unless necessary.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">2. Ao Ech (Frog Pond) – Viet Hai village trekking route</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">This route is longer and more challenging (about 8-10km of forest trail).</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Starting point: Also from the National Park headquarters, or via a side trail off the cross-island road.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Optimization tip: You should ride to the Park gate and trek through the forest to Viet Hai village. At Viet Hai, instead of walking back (which is very time-consuming), hire a boat or speedboat from Viet Hai pier back to Beo Wharf (Cat Ba town). This is a "hidden tip" that lets you complete a full loop without backtracking, saving time on your return to the mainland.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">3. Lien Minh Valley – Butterfly season route</span></h3>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Starting point: Lien Minh hamlet, Tran Chau commune.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">How to reach it: From the cross-island road, you need to turn onto the small road leading into Tran Chau commune. Renting a motorbike is the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tip from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> for this route, since the road into the hamlet is quite narrow and large buses struggle to go deep.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-6.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="600" /></span></p>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Common mistakes that slow you down</span></h2>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Even with the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> in hand, many people still make mistakes:</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Riding a personal motorbike across the Got ferry on weekends: This is the classic mistake. You can get stuck for 2-3 hours under the hot sun. If you must ride a motorbike, set off very early (before 7am), or choose to park it and take the cable car/high-speed boat.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Not checking the tide schedule (for the Viet Hai route): If you plan to take a boat back from Viet Hai, remember that boats depend on the tide. If the water is too low, the boat can't dock and you'll be stranded.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Trusting Google Maps completely: In the forest or on some shortcuts in Cat Ba, Google Maps can give wrong directions. Ask locals or contact tour operators who know the terrain well.</span></li>\r\n
    </ul>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-3.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="599" /></span></p>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Frequently asked questions about getting to Cat Ba trekking</span></h2>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">To give you a fuller picture of the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">, I've compiled the most common questions below:</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Can I bring a bicycle or motorbike on the cable car?</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Currently, the Cat Hai – Phu Long cable car </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">only carries passengers</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> and does not transport motorbikes or bicycles. If you want to bring a mountain bike to combine trekking with cycling, you must take the Got ferry.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">How long is the total travel time from Hai Phong to the forest entrance?</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">If you follow the right route: car to the cable car station (30 min) + cable car (10 min) + taxi to the Park gate (20 min), it takes only about </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">60 minutes</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> to reach the trekking spot. That's much faster than the 2-3 hours of the traditional ferry.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Which season is best for Cat Ba trekking for the smoothest travel?</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Autumn (September - November) and early summer (March - May) are ideal. During these times the weather is dry, the trails aren't slippery, and especially the number of domestic tourists isn't as overwhelming as in June and July, making travel by both ferry and cable car quick.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">How much does the fastest route cost?</span></h3>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">To optimize speed using the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">fastest travel tips from Hai Phong to the Cat Ba trekking spots</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">, the cost will be a bit higher than the ferry. A round-trip cable car ticket is around 300k (depending on the time), plus taxi/bus fare. However, this amount is well worth it to buy back time and a better experience.</span></p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-1.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="553" /></span></p>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">A 5-step super-fast travel process for trekkers</span></h2>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Below is a summary of the standard process to help you picture the route more clearly:</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Step 1: Set off from Hai Phong and head straight to the Cat Hai cable car station (don't turn into the Got ferry terminal).</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Step 2: Buy a ticket and board the cable car, enjoying the 10-minute flight over the bay.</span></li>\r\n

Ask the driver to take you straight to the "Cat Ba National Park Gate."</span></li>\r\n

    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Step 5: Start your trek up Ngu Lam Peak or into Ao Ech right away.</span></li>\r\n

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/meo-di-chuyen-tu-hai-phong-den-cac-diem-trekking-o-cat-ba-nhanh-nhat-4.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="506" /></span></p>\r\n
